title: coccoloba subcordata
intro: Coccoloba subcordata, also known as the Manchineel tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The plant's sap contains a toxic compound called phorbol, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds. The medicinal properties of Coccoloba subcordata are also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to promote wound healing and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the plant's sap can be applied topically to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, or used to make a poultice to treat wounds and minor injuries.
